name: MILES metformin older-adult transcriptomics trial
summary: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled crossover MILES trial testing six weeks each of metformin and placebo in older adults, with skeletal-muscle and adipose biopsies and mixed-meal metabolic testing.
population: Approximately 70-year-old adults with impaired glucose tolerance

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for AMPK and Metabolic Modulators because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers and mechanistic endpoints in Approximately 70-year-old adults with impaired glucose tolerance. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
